content.full_text:
La Sierra, California, United States.
2, July 1,1991; Tuesday.     20:00 pacific time (+8).

Scott Halliburton and Linda Moureaux,
California Fair Political Practices Commission,
lw 428 "J" Street, Suite 800,

Or

United States Post Office Box 807,
Sacramento City, California, United States, 9,58,04-0-807.

Retl-91-283. Scott and Linda. (you may call me Charles)

1, July 1,1991; Monday Our United StatesPostal Service
delivered Cal.F.P.P.C. envelope containing; forms 495,
465, 450, 425, 410, 420, 405; Information Manual B;
Regulations"Section" 18,329; your letter all in perfect
condition.

Thank you all very very much !!!

Yes, I agree this is an informal request for information,
and I think sometimes these requests are necessary
because sometimes to decide whether or not to go ahead
and do something politically actively I must have more
input into my decission process.     So again,I thank you!!!

☐  Please dispatch to me the list of candidates that you
all can.     I presume these include all for Statewide
office of California State, all State Legislative
(State Assembly and State Senate), Any other State
District Offices     please.     Thank you !!!

Again, an informal question.     On the form 501 instructions
it clearly directs"a candidate to file this form if
there intention is to raise or spend any campaign funds..
You Must file form 501...."

☐  Please what is the distinction between a candidate and
a non-controlled committee in supporting that very same
candidate ??

☐  And it may be that it is [illegible] Our law, but can you make
it make sense why I can collect money and spend money
(U.S.$) on an unfiled 501 voter who isqualified for
an office, but may only be babbling on about becoming
a bona fide candidate ???     I think somehow I still
could be construed to be illegal.

I could do this to cheer up one of my friends or somebody.
Or make an impression on my boss or a fellow worker.
You know all of the sudden i'm campaigning for someone
and that should be in office and if I take in and spend
U.S.$10,000.00. then they don't run for office you
all are going to tell me it's O.K. ???

I wonder.     Plus there really are two (2) potential
candidates I'm considering starting up committees for.
I've more questions, but I'll write again sometime.

I am, and reply to,
[signature] AHA. CCPCE.

Mr. Charles Anthony F. Szychowski 7-2-'91.     7-2-'91.

Paid for by
Charles A. Szychowski Committee, 840,330,
1,215 Mobley Avenue,
La Sierra, California, United States, 9,25,05-17,49.

CSics. oneside of a page.     please reply.     And thank you !!!

(PARTICIPATE IN GOVERNMENT.)

[The image shows a document that is extremely faded and difficult to read. Most of the text is illegible or too faint to accurately transcribe. The document appears to be several paragraphs of typed text on a white background, but the contrast is so poor that individual words cannot be reliably determined. There are some scattered dots and marks visible throughout, but no clear, readable text can be confidently transcribed from this image.]

embedding.qa_text: La Sierra, California, United States. 2, July 1, 991; Tuesday. 20:00 pacific time (+8). Scott Hallabrin and Linda Moureaux, California Fair Political Practices Commission, 428 "J" Street, Suite 800, or United States Post Office Box 807, Sacramento City, California, United States, 9,58,04-0,807. Re: I-91-283. Scott and Linda, (you may call me Charles) 1, July 1, 991; Monday Our United States Postal Service delivered Cal.F.P.P.C. envelope containing: forms 495, 465, 450, 425, 410, 420, 405; Information Manual B; Regulations "Section" 18,329; your letter all in perfect condition. Thank you all very very much !!! Yes, I agree this is an informal request for information, and I think sometimes these requests are necessary because sometimes to decide whether or not to go ahead and do something politically actively I must have more input into my decision process. So again, I thank you!!! ☐ Please dispatch to me the list of candidates that you all can. I presume these include all for Statewide Office of California State, all State Legislative (State Assembly and State Senate), Any other State District Offices please. Thank you !!! Again, an informal question. On the form 501 instructions it clearly directs "a candidate to file this form if their intention is to raise or spend any campaign funds... You Must file form 501..." ☐ Please what is the distinction between a candidate and a non-controlled committee in supporting that very same candidate ??? And it may be that it is our law, but can you make it make sense why I can collect money and spend money (U.S.$) on an unfiled 501 voter who is qualified for an office, but may only be babbling on about becoming a bona fide candidate ??? I think somehow I still could be construed to be illegal. I could do this to cheer up one of my friends or somebody. Or make an impression on my boss or a fellow worker. You know all of the sudden I'm campaigning for someone I think should be in office and if I take in and spend U.S.$10,000.00. then they don't run for office you all are going to tell me it's O.K. ??? I wonder. Plus there really are two (2) potential candidates I'm considering starting up committees for. I've more questions, but I'll write again sometime. I am, and reply to, Mr. Charles Anthony Szychowski. 7-2-'91. 7-2-'91. Paid for by Charles A. Szychowski Committee, 840,330, 4,215 Mobley Avenue, La Sierra, California, United States, 9,25,05-17,49. CS:cs. oneside of a page. please reply. And thank you !!! PARTICIPATE IN GOVERNMENT. The image provided is extremely faint and mostly illegible, with very little visible text that can be reliably transcribed. Only a few scattered letters and numbers are partially visible, but the majority of the content cannot be reliably read or transcribed due to the quality and density of the handwriting.
